title: Thinking in Bets
subtitle: Making Smarter Decisions When You Don't Have All the Facts
author: Annie Duke
themes: Separating decision quality from outcome quality
Overcoming cognitive biases (motivated reasoning, hindsight bias, self-serving bias)
Mental time travel for better long-term decisions
Belief updating as a continuous calibration process
The poker mindset as a model for real-world decisions
awards: National Bestseller (New York Times, Wall Street Journal)
excerpt: Every decision is a bet. You never have all the facts, and outcomes are shaped by luck as much as skill. Yet we persistently judge decisions by their results — a trap Annie Duke calls 'resulting.' Drawing on her career as a World Series of Poker champion and her training in cognitive psychology, Duke shows how to separate decision quality from outcome quality, think in probabilities, and build the habits and groups that make us better decision-makers in a fundamentally uncertain world.
